text: Artificial intelligence can make decisions faster than humans, but it also raises important ethical questions. One major concern is bias. If AI is trained on data that contains unfair patterns, it may make biased decisions, for example in hiring or lending. This relates closely to fairness – we need to ensure that AI treats all people equally. Another key issue is privacy. AI systems often collect huge amounts of personal data, so it is important to protect this information and use it ethically. Transparency is also essential; people should understand how AI reaches its conclusions. Ultimately, responsible AI development means considering these risks carefully and creating systems that benefit society while respecting human rights.
